summary: Closed the verifier's isolated helper failure-matrix gap. Missing runtime identity, malformed targets, endpoint unavailability, stale-result replacement, partial HTTP failure after two successful submissions, and preservation of created child IDs are now exercised through the materialized portable helper. Runtime-neutral continuation, full parent/UI projection coherence, and a real AgentRun-to-parent reliability replay remain open.

gapStatuses.evidence: CodexSessionAdapter still owns the bounded terminal-contract continuation loop.
gapStatuses.remaining: Move continuation into replay-safe AgentRun orchestration, branch on the immutable runtime capability snapshot, and cover supported and unsupported runtimes.

gapStatuses.evidence: tests/unit/test_batch_workflows.py::test_materialized_helper_failure_matrix_preserves_authoritative_evidence runs the materialized helper as an isolated subprocess and covers missing identity, malformed targets, unavailable HTTP, failure after two successful POSTs, stale artifact replacement, and retained partial child IDs.
Existing tests cover individual result and dashboard paths but do not jointly assert finishOutcome, mm_state, closeStatus, ledger, diagnostics, queued count, and partial child IDs.
gapStatuses.remaining: Add the verifier-requested parent-boundary and dashboard contract coverage.

gapStatuses.evidence: The MM-1201 reliability fixture still calls evaluate_terminal_evidence directly.
gapStatuses.remaining: Drive AgentRun terminal evaluation and root finalization/projection from the minimized replay fixture.
